title: practical english usage - Part 158
content:
late   later    latest       I  Adjectives ending in -e: cheap  cheaper  cheapest + -er, -est.

tall   taller    tallest Most adjectives: old   older    oldest Adjective  Comparative  Superlative 1 one-syllable        adjectives        (regular     comparison) Longer adjectives have more and most.

-er, -est. Some two-syllable adjectives are similar; others have more and most.

One-syllable adjectives normally have comparatives and superlatives ending in 204 comparative         and   superlative        adjectives grammar •  204  comparative and superlative adjectives -» or more well-known  most well-known well-known better-known     best-known or more good-looking           most good-looking good-looking       better-looking    best-looking comparatives and superlatives.

Some compound adjectives like good-looking or well-known have two possible untidy   untidier / more untidy     untidiest / most untidy unhappy  unhappier / more unhappy  unhappiest / most unhappy exception: they can have forms in -er and -est.

Words like unhappy (the opposites of two-syllable adjectives ending in -y) are an beautiful more beautiful most beautiful practical         more practical         most practical intelligent       more intelligent       most intelligent Adjectives of three or more syllables have more and most.

4 longer     adjectives for a particular two-syllable adjective, check  in a good dictionary.

becoming more common. To  find out the normal comparative and superlative -less), only more/most is possible. In general, the structure with more/most is are both possible. With others (including adjectives ending in -ing, -ed, -Jul and With many two-syllable adjectives (e.g. polite, common), -er/-est and more!most quiet   quieter  quietest clever  cleverer cleverest simple  simpler  simplest narrow narrower narrowest ending in an unstressed vowel, /1/  or /ə(r)/.

Some other two-syllable adjectives can have -er and -est, especially adjectives easy   easier   easiest happy  happier  happiest Adjectives ending in -y have -ier and -iest.

3 two-syllable        adjectives ►     169-170.

Few has two possible comparatives and superlatives: fewer/less and fewest/least.
